leadText: Meowscarada (Japanese: マスカーニャ Masquernya) is a dual-type Grass/Dark Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves from Floragato starting at level 36. It is the final form of Sprigatito.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Meowscarada's camouflaged stem and inactive flower Meowscarada is a humanoid lynx Pokémon with light green fur covering most of its body—stopping just above its long, dark green legs—and a short, fluffy tail. The fur is especially longer around its ears, thighs, and arms, the latter resembling poofy sleeves. Its face has lazy fuchsia eyes and a small fuchsia nose, and is covered in a four-pointed mask-like shape, with a pattern of three green diamonds on it. Its ears are curiously more droopy compared to its pre-Evolutions, its mask instead giving the impression of perked-up cat ears. Each of Meowscarada's hands has three fingers tipped with green claws, as well as a fuchsia paw pad. On its neck is a fuchsia collar resembling flower petals. This collar holds together its cape, which grows from the leaves it had around its neck as Floragato . [ 1 ] The cape is lined with reflective fur, is black on the outside and green on the inside, and splits into two near the base, with both parts of the cape ending in fleur-de-lis shapes. Meowscarada binding a Crocalor with its invisible flower stem Meowscarada is said to be a talented magician. Meowscarada carries a four-petaled green flower, whose stem is hidden by the reflective fur on its cape, creating the illusion of floating. As seen in Pokémon Horizons: The Series , the flower stem can be used to bind opponents in battle. The flower can blossom into a round, pollen-filled flower bomb, with a two-tone green and black zigzag pattern on the outside, and six petals adorning the top. In a battle, Meowscarada uses skillful misdirection to stealthily plant these flower bombs onto various surfaces around its unaware foes before setting them off, catching their opponents off guard. [ 1 ] As mentioned in the Sleep Style Dex , Meowscarada occasionally attempts to perform magic while it sleeps. However, this performance tends to not go as planned. Meowscarada has a proud, showy personality; however, it is also sensitive and prone to jealousy. It can often be very attached to its Trainer, and it will become upset if its Trainer gives attention to other Pokémon. [ 1 ] Meowscarada is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Flower Trick . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Meowscarada evolves from Floragato and is the final form of Sprigatito . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Sprigatito Grass → First Evolution Floragato Grass → Second Evolution Meowscarada Grass Dark ↑ 1.0 1.1 1.2 Meowscarada — Pokémon Scarlet and Pokémon Violet | Official Website

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Meowscarada has the highest base Speed stat of all fully evolved first partner Pokémon . Meowscarada's type combination, Grass / Dark , has the most weaknesses out of all fully evolved first partner Pokémon , at seven (six 2× weaknesses and a 4× weakness). Origin [ edit | edit source ] Meowscarada appears to be based on a lynx (quite possibly the Iberian lynx , given the Paldea region's basis on the Iberian Peninsula ), blended with an illusionist : its high Speed and Dark typing echo sleight of hand , and its category plus Flower Trick nod to the magic trick of producing flowers. Its mask and ears recall the cap of a jester , while diamond markings suggest the Harlequin archetype and French-suited playing cards . The round six-petaled bloom resembles a pomegranate , culturally tied to Spanish regions like Granada , which also connects to its explosive motif via the grenade ’s etymology. It may also draw on the masquerade ball tradition, which began in Burgundy and spread to France and Habsburg Spain , alongside influences from Mardi Gras masks and the cat-faced gnaga . Finally, the magician motif and Dark type evoke the gentleman thief trope, and the Shiny forms of Meowscarada and its pre-evolutions read like their normal colors seen under moonlight, echoing stage magic, masquerades, and phantom-thief heists. Name origin [ edit | edit source ] Meowscarada may be a combination of meow and mascarada (Spanish and Portuguese for masquerade). Masquernya may be a combination of masquerade and ニャー nyā (onomatopoeia for meowing).
